narrative:
Casemiro is Brazil's anchor in defensive midfield. His overall rating of 7.2/10 places him among the top 3% of defensive midfields at this tournament. This rating reflects both his club form over the last two seasons and his current World Cup performances.

Casemiro demonstrates elite defensive prowess, averaging 3.325 tackles, 1.425 interceptions, and 3.087 clearances per 90 minutes at this World Cup. He also offers a surprising offensive threat, scoring 0.237 goals per 90 minutes from an expected goals rate of 0.157. His 0.95 key passes per 90 minutes highlight his ability to initiate attacks.

Casemiro is vital to Brazil's midfield, providing defensive solidity and breaking up opposition play. Scouts and fans should continue to observe his exceptional tackling and interception rates. His ability to exceed his expected goals with 0.237 goals per 90 minutes also makes him a player to watch in crucial moments.
